entries.note: Attempt 3 addressed all two blocking issues from the prior feat-macos-deep-link review that share the same codebase. RC-2: FeatureBoard's item deep-link highlight was using el.setAttribute (DOM mutation outside React reconciliation), which is clobbered when any WS frame triggers a re-render during the 2s pulse. Fixed by replacing with React state (Set<string> highlightedItems) matching AttentionBanner's pattern — data-highlight is now derived from React state, so re-renders cannot overwrite it. RC-3: WorkflowDetailRoute captured the ?attention query param in a ref but relied on WorkflowList's side-effect setSearchParams reset to clear it from the URL. Added an explicit history.replaceState call in a mount-only useEffect that removes the ?attention param immediately after capture, satisfying the spec requirement for deterministic URL cleanup. Also fixed a pre-existing test ambiguity: FeatureBoard's status-filter combobox had aria-label='Filter by status' — identical to WorkflowList's sidebar combobox. Renamed it to 'Filter items by status' and updated the workflow-detail.spec.ts selector accordingly (previously worked around with page.getByRole('main') scoping, now resolved at the source). Added an AC-8 ordering test verifying attention items render oldest-first even when delivered out of order in the snapshot. The full feat-attention-banner implementation spanning attempts 1-3 covers all 8 ACs and 6 RCs.
known_risks: history.replaceState for ?attention cleanup runs in a useEffect([]) (mount-only). If React StrictMode double-invokes effects (dev only), the second invocation is a no-op because the param is already gone after the first replaceState. No production risk.

AttentionBanner deep-link scroll fires in useEffect([]) and reads document.getElementById at mount time. Items are rendered on mount via the items prop passed from the snapshot, so getElementById should succeed. Under slow-network conditions where the snapshot arrives after the component mounts, the scroll would silently no-op.
workflow-detail.spec.ts has an AC-8 ordering test that relies on DOM ID attributes (#attention-item-N). If the ID naming convention changes, the test must be updated.
